TOCHANGE.Wheel.hint: Click any segment to toggle that transgender change aspect. The wheel has no fixed order; you reach Permanence when you are ready.
TOCHANGE.Cross.hint: Left-click a cell = Change (outer to inner); right-click = Damage. The centre is the point of Permanence.
TOCHANGE.Field.deathWarn: Near death: an attribute has 3 damage and Permanence is damaged.
TOCHANGE.Field.changeLogHint: Record the details of every change as it happens.
TOCHANGE.Cards.title: Character Cards (Face / Rising / Falling)
TOCHANGE.Cards.empty: Click the card above to choose
TOCHANGE.Safety.hint: Before play, establish each player's Lines, Veils, and body & mental Tiers together.
TOCHANGE.Safety.bodyTier: Body Tier (parts that must not change)
TOCHANGE.Resolution.title: Resolution
TOCHANGE.Resolution.setupHint: Choose the tested attribute and number of assisters, then draw. Cards drawn = the attribute's undamaged points.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.revealHint: The drawn cards are revealed. Pick one as your fate, or Succumb. Death may only be picked if it is the only card revealed.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.zeroDraw: Draw count is 0: this attribute is depleted, you may only Succumb.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.deathLocked: Death cannot be picked unless it is the only card revealed in this resolution.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.succumbNote: You Succumbed: take one Change on the tested attribute, the action succeeds, all drawn cards shuffle back.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.risingTrigger: Rising card "{card}" revealed: draw one extra card this resolution.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.fallingTrigger: Falling card "{card}" revealed: the first-revealed "{first}" shuffles back.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.towerNote: The Tower: your next resolution reveals two extra cards.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.towerFaceNote: The Tower (Face): all revealed cards (except Death & Temperance) were discarded.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.temperanceNote: Temperance: all discarded cards shuffled back into the deck.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.devilNote: The Devil: you take one Change (applied to the tested attribute); make a wish to the Storyteller about its form.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.deathNote: Death appeared and the resolution succeeded: you take one Change (applied to the tested attribute).
TOCHANGE.Resolution.sunNote: The Sun: the top three cards of the deck are {cards}.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.foolFaceNote: The Fool (Face): resolution failed, the Fool returned from the discard and the deck reshuffled.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.moonPrompt: Draw a new card from the bottom of the deck and use it instead? (Otherwise this is a failure.)
TOCHANGE.Resolution.moonUsed: The Moon: using the bottom card "{card}" instead; both the Moon and that card are discarded.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.judgementPrompt: You decide: does this action succeed?
TOCHANGE.Resolution.worldPrompt: Take one card from the discard pile back into the deck, shuffle, and redraw.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.worldRedraw: The World intervenes and is reborn: the deck is reset, drawing again.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.freeDrawHint: Choose to draw 1-3 cards from the top or bottom of the deck; skips Rising / Falling / Tower rules.
TOCHANGE.Resolution.freeDrawTrigger: Free draw: {count} card(s) from the {end} (resolution rules not applied).
TOCHANGE.Solo.title: Solo Play
TOCHANGE.Solo.disabledHint: Check the box above to enable solo journalling play.
TOCHANGE.Solo.pickStartHint: Choose your Starting card - it decides how your change begins and grants a unique rule.
TOCHANGE.Solo.journalHint: Record today's event and the aftermath of your change.
TOCHANGE.Solo.noStartCard: Please choose a Starting card first.
TOCHANGE.Solo.dailyChangePrompt: Each day you take one Change. Which attribute does it go to?
TOCHANGE.Solo.writeJournal: Write a journal entry for the day.
TOCHANGE.Solo.resetPrompt: Reset the day count back to Day 1?
TOCHANGE.Tables.title: Random Tables
TOCHANGE.Deck.resetPrompt: Shuffle the Future Deck into a fresh pile (all 22 cards back in the deck)?
TOCHANGE.Deck.resetDone: The Future Deck has been reset.
TOCHANGE.DeckViewer.title: Future Deck - Inspector
TOCHANGE.DeckViewer.gmOnly: Only the GM can view the deck.
TOCHANGE.DeckViewer.hint: A live view of the Future Deck's current state, GM only; the deck is not modified.
TOCHANGE.Notify.setupDone: To Change content created (Future Deck, random tables).
TOCHANGE.Notify.noGM: No active GM; the deck state cannot be updated.
TOCHANGE.Notify.wheelChangePending: Pick {n} "{attr}" change aspect(s) on the wheel of the character sheet.
TOCHANGE.Settings.attributeMax.name: Attribute starting maximum
TOCHANGE.Settings.attributeMax.hint: Starting points per attribute for new characters (raise for campaign play). Default 3.
TOCHANGE.Settings.movesMenu.name: Story Moves
TOCHANGE.Settings.movesMenu.label: Configure Story Moves
TOCHANGE.Settings.movesMenu.hint: Choose which Story Moves are active this campaign; they appear on every character sheet as reminders.
TOCHANGE.Move.weakening.hint: Whenever an attribute point is changed, that point is also damaged.
TOCHANGE.Move.strengthening.hint: Players draw extra cards equal to their changed attribute points.
TOCHANGE.Move.conditional.hint: A change only manifests in certain situations (night, water, anger).
TOCHANGE.Move.infectious.hint: Changed points can spread from one player to another under conditions.
TOCHANGE.Move.shared.hint: When any player Succumbs, all players take one Change on that attribute.
TOCHANGE.Move.reversible.hint: When a card that would succeed is revealed, choose to fail to remove one Change.
TOCHANGE.Move.initial.hint: Players begin the story with some attributes already changed or damaged.
TOCHANGE.Move.environmental.hint: Draw fewer or more cards on certain attributes depending on the environment.
TOCHANGE.Move.progressive.hint: At the end of each major scene, take one Change.
TOCHANGE.Move.living.hint: A change offers itself to the player, giving a bonus when cooperated with.
TOCHANGE.Move.rules.hint: Certain player actions carry a Change as their consequence.
TOCHANGE.Move.levels.hint: Reaching a number of Changes or Succumbs reveals a new facet of the story.
TOCHANGE.Move.healing.hint: When restoring attribute damage, take one Change on that attribute.
TOCHANGE.Move.mirrored.hint: When taking a Change on an attribute, the opposite attribute also gains or cancels one.
